title: The Hindu Huddle 2026 LIVE updates: 'Degrees build trust, skills show capability' - Students' Huddle on what matters more in the future
content: Day 1 of The Hindu Huddle in Bengaluru, inaugurated by Karnataka CM D.K. Shivakumar, opened with a session with Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah on 'The role of Kashmir in great power politics'

title: T.N. government firm on protecting State’s quota of medical seats: Health Minister Arunraj
content: Dr. Arunraj’s statement came in response to Leader of the Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin, who sought the immediate intervention of the Chief Minister to prevent 152 vacant super-specialty medical seats from being surrendered to the All India Quota

title: Camera traps installed at 10 locations to track leopard movement in Erode
content: Several cattle kills attributed to leopards have been reported in settlements bordering forest areas, raising concern among farmers who depend on livestock for their livelihood

title: Vanishing shade: Coimbatore’s sparse tree cover falls prey to illegal felling
content: Environmentalists lay bare the glaring gaps in government protection for trees in public places; there is no clarity on the guidelines, Acts, Rules, or G.O.s that govern cutting of such trees, so much so that felling of a lush tree at a reserve site in the city’s Ward 20 went unpunished, they allege

title: Municipal Corporation of Delhi begins sealing ‘unauthorised’ establishments in popular Hauz Khas Village, Saidulajab
content: According to officials, at least four teams of Municipal Corporation of Delhi comprising junior and assistant engineers have been deployed for the exercise

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly session to begin with Governor’s address on June 18
content: Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar says no decision had been taken with respect to the petitions submitted by two groups of MLAs of the AIADMK legislature party, who later came to a truce

title: Farmer trampled to death by wild elephant in Thalavadi hills
content: The latest fatality comes a day after a construction worker was killed in an elephant attack in the Kadambur Hills on Wednesday night

title: Chennai-based CIBA scientists develop soil-friendly fertilisers from fish waste
content: The products address the twin challenges of scientific fish waste disposal and the growing need for eco-friendly nutrient sources that enhance soil fertility and agricultural productivity

title: Assam Cabinet undergoes expansion, 12 MLAs sworn in
content: Governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya administered the oath of office and secrecy to the MLAs at a function at the Jyoti-Bishnu Antarjatik Kala Mandir in Guwahati

title: In pictures: The Hindu Huddle 2026 | Day 1
content: The Hindu Huddle on June 5 and 6, plays host to a host of thought-provoking talks, panel discussions and conversations. Here's a look at how the event unfolded.

title: Woman stabbed to death by spurned partner in Mohali office
content: The assailant tried to take his own life after attacking the victim, and both were rushed to a hospital

title: Holding the flock together: Mamata reaches out to rebels as TMC battles further fragmentation
content: The calls come at a time when the TMC founder finds herself in the unfamiliar position of fighting to retain leaders she had once handpicked and politically nurtured

title: Annamalai floats ‘political movement’, to fight next general election in Tamil Nadu
content: The political movement is inspired by the ideals of former President A.P.J. Abdul Kalam. No animosity against the BJP, says Annamalai who is the former president of BJP’s Tamil Nadu unit

title: Kerala rains: IMD sounds red alert for five districts on June 6
content: Rest of Kerala has been put on orange alert, except Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, and Alappuzha, where very heavy rain is likely

title: White Paper on Kerala finances moots regulatory commission for Kochi metro user charges
content: The document notes that Kochi Metro registers a monthly loss of ₹35 crore. Notwithstanding these losses, the White Paper proposes metro projects in two more cities — Thiruvananthapuram and Kozhikode

title: The Hindu Huddle on Campus: ‘Social media protects people from being held accountable for actions’
content: Moderator Vishu Dev C.H., a faculty member from Amity School for Communication Enhancement and Transformation, pointed to concerns around anxiety, depression, distractions, and mental health and well-being

title: Kharge files nomination papers for Rajya Sabha polls from Karnataka
content: Rahul Gandhi, D.K. Shivakumar, Siddaramaiah, B.K. Hariprasad accompany Mallikarjun Kharge, who is seeking re-election

title: Case against relegating Tamil Thai Vazhthu to third place during T.N. CM Vijay’s swearing-in ceremony withdrawn
content: Judges say they cannot entertain the petition without there being any challenge to the Union Home Ministry’s circular regarding the playing of the national song and national anthem during events attended by the President/Governors

title: ‘Nilavilakku’ row in Kerala: Samastha clarifies position, says Muslims should avoid religious rituals of other faiths
content: Samastha meeting noted that if a Muslim participates in such a practice while accepting or endorsing the beliefs that are contrary to Islamic teachings, the act could amount to leaving the fold of Islam

title: Dharmendra Pradhan reviews draft mental health policy for schools; says well-being key to quality education
content: The policy is expected to focus on strengthening mental health support systems in schools, including counselling and early identification of stress among students

title: Compensatory afforestation for biodiversity loss in Nicobar is bogus argument: Jairam Ramesh
content: ‘in India, unlike in many other parts of the world, when we protect nature, you also protect culture’

title: Torso of unidentified man found in suitcase at Chennai’s Perambur railway station
content: Police are verifying missing persons complaints and coordinating with police stations across Chennai and neighbouring districts to identify the deceased

title: DVAC conducts searches at 60 Sub-Registrar Offices across Tamil Nadu
content: The teams carried out searches to detect unaccounted cash and identify any irregularities in the registration process

title: Abrogation of Article 370 was the biggest policy mistake by India, says Omar Abdullah
content: ‘Give us statehood, give a timeline and parameters on which it will be decided,’ says the Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister

title: Pakistan ‘forever grateful’ to Trump for India ceasefire intervention: PM Sharif
content: At an event marking the 250th anniversary of American independence, Shehbaz Sharif described Pakistan-U.S. ties as a "true and special relationship" spanning nearly eight decades

title: India-China relations have improved from ‘reset and fresh start’ to ‘new level’: Chinese envoy Xu Feihong
content: Chinese Ambassador to India Xu Feihong in conversation with Suhasini Haidar during the session ‘India-China tango: Asian powers shaping the emerging world order’

title: TCS Nashik case: Complainant says she was made to watch videos of Pakistani Islamic clerics
content: The victim said the accused orchestrated a calculated psychological and religious manipulation campaign, exploiting her vulnerability under the guise of reducing her ‘mental stress’

title: Madras High Court refuses to stay circular mandating private schools in T.N. to display fee structure on notice boards
content: Justice M. Dhandapani rejects the plea for interim stay and says, he would instead take up the main case for final disposal after two weeks

title: Vijay-led Tamil Nadu Cabinet outlines 436 vision statements titled ‘Vetri Tamizhagam’
content: The vision document encompasses many of the poll promises made by the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam in the run-up to the 2026 Assembly elections

title: Delhi HC refuses urgent hearing on plea against Cockroach Janta Party protest
content: The petitioner, in his plea, sought deployment of "immediate preventive, regulatory, and crowd-control measures" at the IGI Airport, metro stations, and highway entry points

title: At 76, Naidu pedals 5.5 km of midday heat for a greener Visakhapatnam
content: On World Environment Day, the 76-year-old Chief Minister swaps his car for an e-bicycle, riding 5.5 km through Visakhapatnam on a hot day to press for more green cover

title: BJP chief Nitin Nabin accepts Annamalai’s resignation from party’s membership
content: The developments comes three days after Mr. Annamalai held meetings with Union Home Minister Amit Shah, Mr. Nabin, and national general secretary of the party B.L. Santhosh and submitted his resignation letter to the party leadership on June 2

title: Naidu, four Union Ministers open Visakhapatnam seafood meet; India eyes ₹1 lakh crore exports
content: Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu and four Union Ministers open the two-day Visakhapatnam workshop targeting ₹1 lakh crore in annual seafood exports, up from a record ₹73,890.46 crore in 2025-26

title: Delhi Gymkhana Club: A battle of public and private rights
content: Members of the club include the National Capital’s high-heeled, such as politicians, business tycoons, and celebrities.

title: World Environment Day: PM Modi calls for renewed commitment to sustainable growth
content: World Environment Day is celebrated annually on June 5 to raise awareness and encourage action for environmental protection

title: India’s economic growth depends on protecting its fragile environment
content: India has invested considerably in predicting the weather and the climate and the results have been paying off handsomely in terms of protecting lives and livelihoods. This particular mechanism is crucial to sustain economic growth and stay on track for ‘Viksit Bharat’

title: Bihar government withdraws Z-plus security cover of Lalu Prasad, Rabri Devi
content: According to a notification, former Chief Ministers Lalu Prasad and Rabri Devi will now be provided security cover by the Bihar Special Armed Police

title: Cross-border illegal arms, drugs network unearthed in Punjab; four held
content: DGP Gaurav Yadav said the Amritsar Commissionerate Police also recovered 1.035 kg heroin, five sophisticated pistols, two live cartridges and ₹5 lakh drug money

title: INDIA bloc dead and buried, exists only on paper after DMK boycott: BJP
content: The decision is a continuation of its stand against the Congress ever since the national party joined hands with the TVK, which won the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections

title: How this ‘tree man’ from Kerala has planted over 20,000 trees in 26 years
content: Palakkad native M. Shyamkumars’ work extends beyond Kerala, with trees planted in Puducherry and Kavaratti

title: Ex-minister Aroop Biswas’s brother held in alleged extortion in Tollygunge film industry
content: Swarup Biswas, the former president of the Federation of Cine Technicians & Workers of Eastern India (FCTWEI), was arrested based on an FIR lodged by the female make-up artist who accused him of blacklisting her for two years and asked for money and sexual favour to get work

title: Sightsavers India opens 2026 fellowship for ophthalmologists
content: The 18-month programme is designed to equip early-career ophthalmologists with advanced clinical, surgical, and leadership skills while preparing them to deliver quality eye care in areas of high need

title: Kerala HC rejects CMRL appeal, allows ED to continue probe linked to Pinarayi’s daughter T. Veena
content: Court holds that ED is not barred from initiating civil action with regard to the case, even without registration of FIR or a complaint in the predicate offence

title: Fire breaks out at Noida complex, 15 people rescued; short circuit suspected
content: Chief Fire Officer Pradeep Kumar said the fire was reported at a complex near Shatabdi Vihar under Sector 24 police station limits

title: Below-normal monsoon for Andhra Pradesh, but short, heavy spells likely: IMD
content: The State is likely to see more thunderstorms, lightning and strong winds during the southwest monsoon, affecting the agricultural activities, says IMD senior scientist

title: Deputy Collector arrested for alleged sexual assault in Madhya Pradesh
content: According to the police, a 30-year-old woman gave a complaint in the Civil Lines police station in Morena city against Deputy Collector Arvind Mahour, alleging that he had sexually exploited her on multiple occasions by promising to marry her.

title: India condemns attack on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon; seeks accountability
content: Sergeant Milovan Jovanovic, a Serbian peacekeeper serving with the UNIFIL, was killed when a mortar struck a UN position near Marji'yun on June 3

title: Saffron offshoots: Leaders who quit BJP to start their own parties
content: From Shankersinh Vaghela in Gujarat to K Annamalai in Tamil Nadu, BJP has seen many leaders part ways. Most of them returned to the saffron folds and quit from active politics.

title: NEET-UG paper leak row: Digvijaya Singh urges PM Modi to issue white paper on NTA exam irregularities and action taken
content: The NEET (UG) 2026 exam, held on May 3 by the NTA, was cancelled on May 12 amid allegations of paper leak which is now being probed by the CBI

title: T. G. Bharath seeks TTD assistance for Kurnool’s Gayatri Goshala cow shelter
content: The Industries Minister asks the Endowments Minister to secure TTD support for the State’s largest goshala, which houses more than 1,200 cows

title: 'Don't want to burden top leadership': What Annamalai told BJP in his resignation letter
content: Former Tamil Nadu BJP chief K Annamalai has resigned to launch a new political movement, aiming to end "cult and dynastic" politics. Annamalai expressed a desire to change the notion of politics and connect with local aspirations, stating national parties often fail to speak the language of Tamil Nadu's people.

title: More trouble brewing for Mamata's TMC? Expelled MLA Ritabrata makes fresh claim
content: Expelled TMC leader Ritabrata Banerjee expressed confidence that support for his rebel faction will grow, claiming a significant number of MLAs are backing him. He also appealed to CM Suvendu Adhikari to take action against BJP leader Priyanka Tibrewal for protesting outside a fellow rebel's residence.

title: ‘Front half of car was under a truck’: Clarke reveals terrifying crash after IPL final
content: Former Australia captain Michael Clarke was involved in a serious road accident in India shortly after the IPL 2026 final. His vehicle crashed into a semi-trailer, leaving him with bruises and the car written off. Clarke expressed gratitude for the support from locals and the BCCI following the frightening incident.

title: Will India finally get plastic banknotes? RBI's polymer currency plan explained
content: The Reserve Bank of India is considering introducing polymer currency notes, a move that could enhance durability and security. Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated the proposal is in its preliminary stages, with the central bank examining its advantages and disadvantages. This initiative aims to address the rapid deterioration of existing paper notes and combat fake currency circulation.

title: Musk tells Jamie Dimon that America faces a catastrophic 'zero memory fab' crisis
content: Elon Musk revealed SpaceX's IPO plans, driven by capital-intensive projects like deploying 100,000 satellites and orbital AI data centers. He emphasized the critical threat of the US lacking domestic high-volume computer memory chip manufacturing, warning of catastrophic consequences for technological future and national security.

title: Zorawar tank: The made-in-India war machine built to dominate China on the LAC
content: India's defence industry has achieved a significant milestone with the indigenous Zorawar light tank, developed in just 19 months for high-altitude Himalayan warfare. Designed to counter China's Type 15, this 25-tonne tank boasts a 105mm gun and advanced missile capabilities.  The army plans an initial order of 59 units, with induction expected in 2027.

title: Don’t have courage to appear for NEET again, 18-year-old writes, then dies by suicide
content: A 18-year-old aspiring doctor in Nagpur died by suicide following the NEET re-examination announcement, citing immense pressure and shattered dreams after a paper leak. Her family had borrowed heavily for her coaching. Opposition leaders have linked her tragic death to systemic failures and corruption within the examination process.

title: Video shows effects of intense fire that forced the world's most powerful warship to return to port
content: A stunning video uncovers the alarming reality of a fire onboard the USS Gerald R. Ford, the latest pride of the US Navy, challenging previous accounts. Crew members recounted a harrowing struggle for life as fire suppression measures faltered, leading to severe destruction in living quarters and infrastructure.

title: Microsoft HR head Amy Coleman to employees: I want to be transparent about how things are feeling
content: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman shared employee survey results, highlighting increased employee "energized" and "empowered" feelings, alongside strengths in security and inclusion. However, challenges remain in opportunities for experience broadening, productivity support, and clarity on work's organizational connection. Coleman pledged greater transparency and communication amidst intense change.

title: After vice-captaincy snub, Gambhir sends 'respect situation' warning to Pant
content: Pant was removed as India's Test vice-captain when the BCCI announced the squad for the one-off Test against Afghanistan last month

title: Who is Gauri Spratt? Aamir Khan's would be third wife and the mother of six-year-old son
content: Aamir Khan is set to marry his longtime girlfriend Gauri Spratt on July 5 in what will be his third marriage. Gauri, a fashion professional and mother of a six-year-old son, has been associated with Aamir Khan Films. The two reconnected after knowing each other for 25 years.

title: As China and Pakistan move forward with their fifth generation plans, India's two short term options
content: As India grapples with a shrinking air force fleet and setbacks in its advanced fighter jet initiatives, Russia has stepped in with a compelling proposition: the Su-57 fighter jets, offered with technology transfer. This comes at a crucial time as China expands its own stealth fighter capabilities.

title: Google job cuts: Company says evaluating internal structures
content: Google has recently laid off employees within its Cloud division, impacting its Threat Intelligence Group and Mandiant. This move, mirroring broader tech industry trends, sees the company reallocating resources towards artificial intelligence development. Google AI CEO Demis Hassabis, however, believes companies should leverage AI-driven productivity gains to expand, not reduce, their workforce.

title: 'Nothing to worry': DKS in damage-control mode after 'closest of friend' quits cabinet
content: Karnataka chief minister DK Shivakumar has assured that there is "nothing to worry" regarding the resignation of minister Ramalinga Reddy. Reddy, a close friend and senior leader, expressed dissatisfaction with his allocated portfolio and sought a different ministerial post. Shivakumar stated he would discuss the matter with Reddy to resolve the issue.

title: 'We should end cult & dynastic politics': Annamalai quits BJP, announces new 'political movement'
content: Former BJP leader K Annamalai has launched a new political movement, the APJ Abdul Kalam Centre for Ethics and Politics, based in Coimbatore. He announced his intention to contest future Assembly elections and stressed the need for systemic political reform, aiming to end 'cult politics' and dynastic rule.

title: 'Hasn’t got fair chance': Gambhir confirms GT batter at No.3 for Afghanistan Test
content: The number three position has remained unsettled for India in Test cricket since Cheteshwar Pujara moved out of the side. Since then, seven different batters have been tried at one-drop, but none have managed to make the spot their own. Several of them were also not given an extended run in the role.

title: PM Modi welcomes AirTrunk's Rs 3 lakh crore investment plan in India
content: Blackstone-backed AirTrunk plans a massive Rs 3 lakh crore investment in India by 2030 to boost digital infrastructure, including data centres and AI capacity. Prime Minister Modi welcomed the move, highlighting its potential to strengthen India's global position in cloud computing and AI, create jobs, and drive innovation-led growth.

title: ‘If there's an opportunity ...’: Gambhir backs ICC's pink-ball rule for bad light
content: India head coach Gautam Gambhir supports the ICC's new rule allowing a pink ball under floodlights to combat bad light in Test matches. He believes this proactive measure will help ensure matches reach a conclusion, especially in crucial World Test Championship scenarios, preventing teams from being denied results due to weather.

title: What makes the S-400 India's most powerful air defence system and why it matters to Pakistan
content: India's air defence capabilities have been significantly bolstered by the S-400 system, a formidable shield against aerial threats. Its advanced radar and missile technology allows for the interception of aircraft and ballistic missiles at extreme ranges, far surpassing Western counterparts. This strategic asset provides crucial coverage against regional adversaries, enhancing national security.

title: Got the keys, but is the home really yours? Registry vs possession explained
content: Many homebuyers mistakenly believe physical possession of a property equates to legal ownership. However, possession merely grants access, while registration under the Registration Act, 1908, formally transfers title and records you as the rightful owner. Without registration, you lack legal rights to sell, mortgage, or defend your claim. Both possession and registry are crucial for complete ownership.

title: 'There is no loss': Tamil Nadu BJP chief as his predecessor Annamalai quits party
content: Tamil Nadu BJP chief Nainar Nagendran dismissed any loss following K. Annamalai's exit, who has launched a new political movement, the APJ Abdul Kalam Centre for Ethics and Politics. Annamalai aims to foster systemic change, ending cult and dynastic politics, and encouraging technocrats. His departure follows speculation after the BJP's performance in recent elections.

title: ‘Sooryavanshi has ticked all the boxes’: Ex-selector says 15-yr-old deserves India cap
content: Former India selector Devang Gandhi advocates for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's immediate India T20I debut following his stellar IPL 2026. Gandhi highlighted the young batsman's remarkable strike rate and adaptability, suggesting he possesses the potential to trouble even Test bowlers.  He emphasised Sooryavanshi's maturity and all-around game, calling him a 'nightmare' for opposition attacks.

title: Rupee gains sharply as RBI unveils steps to attract foreign investors
content: Currency market participants said investor confidence improved following the RBI's policy announcements, particularly after the central bank emphasised that India's foreign exchange reserves remain strong enough to cushion the economy against external disruptions.

title: ‘Rohit has to do a lot of hard work’: Ex-selector says Virat's WC spot is beyond doubt
content: Former selector Saba Karim believes Virat Kohli is a certainty for the 2027 ODI World Cup due to his consistent performances and fitness. In contrast, Rohit Sharma faces significant pressure to improve his fitness and batting to be considered for the tournament. Kohli's evolving strike rate and commitment impress selectors, while Rohit's recent IPL form raises concerns.

title: 'In best of families...': Netanyahu responds to Trump's 'crazy' remark
content: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu acknowledged 'tactical disagreements' with US President Trump over regional conflicts but emphasized their shared goals, particularly countering Iran's nuclear ambitions and Hezbollah. Despite recent heated exchanges, Netanyahu hailed Trump as Israel's 'greatest friend,' citing his strong support for the nation. Both leaders aim for regional stability.

title: Watch: Rahul scuba dives among Great Nicobar's coral reefs; raises concerns over mega project
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized the Great Nicobar development project, alleging it prioritizes commercial interests over the island's ecology and local communities. He highlighted concerns about tribal land rights violations, inadequate compensation for settlers, and the potential felling of 1.5 crore trees. Gandhi proposed expanding INS Baaz as a strategic alternative and advocated for sustainable tourism and conservation.

title: 'Find room for him': Sooryavanshi tipped to open for India in Tests after IPL heroics
content: During a discussion on Sky Sports on the opening day of the first Test between England and New Zealand on Thursday, the idea of Sooryavanshi opening in Tests was raised.

title: Physiotherapist's 3:50 am wake-up, patient visits and UPSC study routine leaves internet stunned
content: A physiotherapist's rigorous daily schedule, starting at 3:50 am to balance UPSC exam preparation, patient care, and household duties, has captivated social media. Her dedication to a demanding routine, documented in a viral video, has inspired many, while others express awe and concern about her ability to sustain such intensity.

title: CBSE receives over 70,000 re-evaluation requests as portal withstands cyberattack attempts
content: CBSE’s post-result grievance portal has received over 70,000 applications for verification and re-evaluation within days of launch, even as it faced repeated cyberattack attempts. The Board reported 7,314 verification and 63,119 re-evaluation requests. Despite a DDoS attack and millions of access requests, security systems including WAF and DDoS mitigation kept the portal functional. The facility supports Class 12 students reviewing scanned answer books under the new On-Screen Marking system and post-result process rollout framework structure.

title: Not Anderson or Broad: Robinson becomes 1st bowler in England's Test history to ...
content: Robinson, playing his first Test since February 2024, finished the day with figures of 4/10 from six overs as New Zealand ended the day still trailing England's first-innings total of 140.

title: YouTuber Jesse Ridgway and wife share decision after down syndrome diagnosis
content: YouTuber Jesse Ridgway and his wife Ashley shared a difficult personal update. They learned their unborn child had Down syndrome during pregnancy. This led to a deeply emotional decision. The couple is now focusing on recovery and supporting each other. They expressed gratitude for fan support during this challenging time. Ridgway detailed the medical considerations that influenced their choice.

title: 'Leaving my fate in the hands of Constitution': CJP founder heads to India
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke is en route to India for a protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Dipke cites alleged irregularities in national examinations like NEET and CUET, impacting millions of students. He plans a peaceful, lawful demonstration after arriving on June 6.

title: RERA FAQs: 25 questions every homebuyer should know the answers to
content: India's RERA Act has revolutionized real estate by mandating project registration, transparent disclosures, and escrow accounts to prevent fund diversion. Developers now face strict penalties for delays and violations, while buyers gain rights to refunds and interest. This landmark legislation ensures accountability, protecting millions of homebuyers from past uncertainties and malpractices.

title: Only 32 TMC rebels attend meet; 16 back Mamata as 'chairperson'
content: Trinamool Congress faces internal turmoil following a party split, with Lok Sabha MPs reportedly receiving overtures to abandon the Mamata Banerjee-Abhishek Banerjee leadership. Meanwhile, a faction of 58 MLAs has shown signs of wavering, with some demanding Mamata remain party chairperson. The Speaker's ruling invalidating expulsions further complicates the situation.

title: Days after taking oath, 'humilated' Ramalinga Reddy quits Karnataka cabinet; cites 'DKS U-turn'
content: Karnataka minister R Ramalinga Reddy resigned just two days after his oath, citing strong dissatisfaction with his allocated portfolio. He expressed disappointment, stating Chief Minister DK Shivakumar reneged on an assurance regarding the Bengaluru development department. Reddy affirmed his continued loyalty to the Congress party, emphasizing he would not work against his conscience.

title: Jewar airport, Heritage city & more: The key projects driving YEIDA's Master Plan 2041
content: With an eye on the future, YEIDA's Masterplan 2041 is set to revolutionize the vicinity of the upcoming Noida International Airport. This bold strategy aims to craft a prominent global destination, inclusive of an airport city, a unique investment zone, a state-of-the-art Olympic Park for athletic events, and a vibrant international film city.

title: Govt exempts capital gains tax on FIIs' govt bond investments
content: The move is aimed at making Indian debt markets more attractive to overseas investors while helping shield the economy from the effects of the continuing Iran conflict.

title: IIM Indore to study Vaibhav Suryavanshi: Is he the first to inspire academic research? Not quite
content: IIM Indore is launching a study on 15-year-old Vaibhav Suryavanshi, who dominated IPL 2026. The research will explore his talent development, resilience, and the support systems behind his success. This follows a trend of academic institutions examining elite athletes like MS Dhoni and Michael Jordan to understand leadership and sustained excellence

title: 92.62m! Sri Lanka's Tharanga goes past Neeraj Chopra with Asia's second-best throw
content: ​​His throw now trails only Arshad Nadeem's Olympic gold-winning mark of 92.97m from Paris 2024 in the Asian all-time list.

title: Video: Massive fire breaks out on 12th floor of luxury high-rise in Noida
content: A massive fire erupted on the 12th floor of an apartment in Noida's IVY County Society, Sector 75, causing panic among residents. Firefighters are on the scene battling the intense blaze and preventing its spread. The cause of the fire is currently unknown and under investigation.

title: 'India ke liye 15 saal khelna hai': DC's 'ziddi boy' Madhav Tiwari chases big dream
content: Emerging talent Madhav Tiwari, a 22-year-old from Madhya Pradesh, made a significant Indian Premier League debut for Delhi Capitals in 2026 season, showcasing his all-round abilities with both bat and ball.  Despite limited prior experience, Tiwari impressed by taking crucial wickets and playing a match-winning cameo, fueling his ambition to represent India.

title: 'I like your Prime Minister a lot': Trump praises PM Modi as India-US trade talks continue
content: Momentum towards a deal has strengthened in recent weeks. US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor said the negotiations were nearing completion, with only a limited number of issues still unresolved. Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal has similarly indicated that most matters have been settled, with discussions now centred on finalising the remaining details before the first phase of the agreement is unveiled.

title: Annamalai Resignation Live Updates: "A New Path, A New Political Movement": K Annamalai After BJP Exit
content: Annamalai Resignation Live Updates: Annamalai travelled to Delhi to meet with party leadership amid speculation about his potential resignation and plans to establish a new political party.

title: 'Give Me Last Chance': Indian Man Caught Grooming 14-Year-Old In UK
content: When confronted, the Indian man accepted sending sexually explicit texts to the girl, despite knowing she was 14 years old.

title: "Furnace": Madhya Pradesh Hospital's Burn Ward Patients Suffering In Heat
content: The air conditioners in the ICU, Operation Theatre and patient wards have been lying defunct for months.

title: Videos of People Descending Into NYC Sewers Spark Investigation
content: Aki Jakupovic, who owns an auto detailing shop in the area, was working overnight when he noticed three men dressed in waders approaching a nearby manhole.

title: Maharashtra Sex Ratio At Birth Raises Alarm Over Urban-Rural Divide
content: At 899 girls per 1,000 boys, Maharashtra's sex ratio indicated a gender imbalance at birth continues to be a concern.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Trump's 'Forced Labour' Tariff Charge May Really Be About Something Else
content: After the US Supreme Court curtailed the use of emergency powers for reciprocal tariffs, the Trump administration is searching for firmer legal ground.

title: Government Bond Returns Now Tax-Free For Foreign Investors
content: Under the revised framework, the long-term capital gains (LTCG) tax on government securities has been reduced from 12.5 per cent to zero.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 250 Points Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent was trading around $95 a barrel. Meanwhile, WTI was under $93 a barrel.

title: Delhi Hotel Owner Spent 15 Days In Tihar In 2025 For Fake Passports, Aadhaar Cards
content: Lavkesh Bajaj helped Bangladeshi nationals secure passport Indian identity documents on his residential address in exchange for money.

title: Delhi University Professor Living Alone Found Murdered In Her Locked Flat
content: Preliminary investigation indicate that she was hit on the head with a heavy object, the police said. There is a deep injury on the head, and the veins in her wrist were found severed.

title: K Annamalai Resigns From BJP: Full Text Of His Letter
content: K Annamalai said in his resignation letter that the time had come for him to reassess his purpose of entering politics.

title: US-Iran Live Updates: "Would Be Honoured To Meet": Trump On Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei
content: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

title: Bihar Government Withdraws Z-Plus Security Of Lalu Yadav, Rabri Devi
content: Bihar government withdraws Z-plus security cover for Lalu Yadav and Rabri Devi after VIP security review.

title: TISS Post-Graduate Among Dead In Delhi Hotel Fire. Was Going To Start 1st Job
content: Shrutika Baranwal was one of 21 people killed on Wednesday when a fast-moving fire tore through a bed-and-breakfast facility in Hauz Rani, South Delhi.

title: 'Pressuring PM Modi To Limit Ties With Russia Harms Global Relations': Putin
content: The Russian leader also said that Moscow would continue to deepen ties with India despite geopolitical tensions surrounding the country's global engagements.

title: 'Singam' K Annamalai: BJP's Tamil Nadu Star Rose Fast, Fell Faster
content: K Annamalai flew down to Delhi, met party chief Nitin Nabin on Tuesday and said he wants to end the partnership on "cordial terms".

title: UP’s green renaissance: A future where growth & nature will thrive
content: rising global temperatures, erratic monsoons, drying rivers, declining groundwater resources, air pollution, and loss of biodiversity have emerged as serious threats to human life . floods, droughts, heatwaves, and other extreme weather events are becoming increasingly frequent across different parts of the world . at such a critical juncture, Uttar Pradesh has chosen a new path that seeks a balance between development and environmental sustainability .
